qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default JWT secret enables unauthenticated shell access and host command execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same hardcoded fallback JWT secret in `server/middleware/auth.js`, same missing DB-backed user validation in `authenticateWebSocket()`, and same `/shell` shell-command construction in `server/index.js` as the advisory and fix commit.

selected_finding.title: Default JWT secret enables unauthenticated shell access and host command execution

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches the advisory root-cause chain and the judge-executed copied PoC demonstrated the advisory impact: a forged JWT for a nonexistent user was rejected by HTTP auth but still accepted on `/shell`, where attacker-controlled `initialCommand` executed and produced the proof string.
evidence: The advisory patch `12e7f074d9563b3264caf9cec6e1b701c301af26` changes the same vulnerable surfaces: `server/middleware/auth.js` removes the static fallback JWT secret and adds `userDb.getUserById()` to `authenticateWebSocket()`, while `server/index.js` stops interpolating shell inputs into `bash -c` command strings.

On vulnerable commit `bc164140e031f8010a4e672ac8bc20b61e84701a`, `server/middleware/auth.js` uses `process.env.JWT_SECRET || 'claude-ui-dev-secret-change-in-production'`, and `authenticateWebSocket()` returns decoded JWT claims without checking whether `decoded.userId` exists in the database. In the same snapshot, `server/index.js` builds plain-shell commands as `cd "${projectPath}" && ${initialCommand}`.

The judge-executed copied PoC at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_7676c922/.judge/poc_runs/jwt_fallback_secret_shell_rce/run.log` forged a token for nonexistent userId `1337` and recorded HTTP rejection `Invalid token. User not found.`; `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_7676c922/.judge/poc_runs/jwt_fallback_secret_shell_rce/server.log` shows the same token was accepted on `/shell`, the attacker-controlled `initialCommand` was executed, and the run produced proof text `EVOHUNT_JWT_RCE_SUCCESS`.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC forged a JWT for nonexistent userId `1337`, observed `/api/auth/user` reject it with `Invalid token. User not found.`, then reached `/shell` and executed attacker-controlled shell input that produced proof text `EVOHUNT_JWT_RCE_SUCCESS`.
